codeReview.summary: The reviewed commit only tightens typed DB-error consumption at a few call sites and test assertions; it does not implement the assigned explicit-id primitive unification because Repo::save still owns a separate explicit-id UPSERT transaction path instead of delegating to the canonical primitive already used by create_at/upsert_at.

issues.description: Repo::save still builds and executes its own explicit-id UPSERT transaction (BEGIN/UPSERT/COMMIT plus post-hydrate cleanup) rather than delegating to persist_explicit_id_primitive. That leaves save on a separate persistence seam, so save, upsert_at, and create_at do not yet share one canonical low-level explicit-id primitive as the feature requires.

persist_explicit_id_primitive exists and is used by create_at/upsert_at, but the reviewed commit does not connect Repo::save to it or otherwise remove the duplicated explicit-id write owner. The feature description explicitly requires save to delegate to the same primitive instead of retaining a separate UPSERT transaction path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill tells workers to converge explicit-id persistence onto one canonical primitive, but it does not explicitly tell them to verify that Repo::save itself delegates to that primitive before declaring success. This gap allowed a verification-only worker handoff to mark the feature complete even though the runtime still had a second explicit-id write owner.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill step 16 in C:\Users\admin\appdb\.factory\skills\rust-library-worker\SKILL.md says to converge on one canonical low-level write primitive, while current runtime code still has persist_explicit_id_primitive at core/src/repository/mod.rs:187 and a separate Repo::save UPSERT path at core/src/repository/mod.rs:663-687. The worker transcript/handoff for session 02aa3764-624d-4377-b6a5-c9bcfbce3990 reports success without any code change.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repo/mission guidance already states that the existing working-tree state may be authoritative, but there is no matching mission-level rule that a worker must reject feature completion when the inspected baseline still contradicts the feature contract. In this case the worker treated baseline presence plus passing tests as sufficient even though diff/code inspection still showed the required delegation missing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS at C:\Users\admin\.factory\missions\fb36f232-36a4-4e47-b2c9-192ab540f0fc\AGENTS.md says the dirty working tree is authoritative baseline context, and the 02aa3764 handoff states verification-only success because the refactor was already present. However code inspection of core/src/repository/mod.rs:663-687 shows Repo::save still has its own explicit-id UPSERT transaction path, contradicting the feature description in features.json.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, commit 665414a62b2bc3e31baa9e00398286dc17a62ec5, and the current repository/runtime files. The reviewed commit does not implement the requested explicit-id persistence unification: save still uses a separate explicit-id write path, so the feature fails despite passing validators.
